text:
[2026-05-20 16:47] Brunel вҖ” diagnosis package received and reviewed. Solid work, headline correct: substrate already designed for multi-key, only gap is missing `SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_2` in host-side `.env`. Approving the Option A direction.

TWO PO DIRECTIVES that reshape the dispatch:

1. **Topology confirmed: remote Linux host, SSH-as-host-user model.** Whole workflow is in Hopper's scope. The `.env` lives on the bare-metal host next to the compose file. Your D3 container-user SSH (`ai-teams@100.96.54.170:2222`) is correct for key extraction. But for `.env` ops and compose ops we need the SSH-as-host-user shape (`ssh dev@<apex-host>` style) вҖ” which you haven't surfaced yet. Pull this from `~/bin/rc-deployments.json` + `~/bin/rc-connect.ps1`.

2. **NO container rebuild this dispatch.** PO direction verbatim: "please do not test container rebuild вҖ” apex team should approve maintenance window first." This means: Phase 1 is preparation-only. Phase 2 (Tier D recreate + verification) is a SEPARATE future dispatch after apex team greenlights a maintenance window. We are not testing the fix end-to-end this round.

REVISED DELIVERABLE вҖ” recompose the Hopper dispatch package as PREPARATION-ONLY:

Phase 1 scope (this dispatch):
- P1.1 [Tier R, container-user SSH port 2222] Extract Aleksandr's pubkey from live container's `authorized_keys`. Same as your D3.
- P1.2 [Tier R, host-user SSH] Locate `.env` on the bare-metal host. Confirm path. (Don't read its contents to log вҖ” just confirm the file exists and is the right one.)
- P1.3 [Tier M, host-user SSH] Backup `.env` to `.env.bak.2026-05-20` (or similar timestamped name). The backup is the rollback artifact even though we're not recreating yet вҖ” it documents the pre-edit state.
- P1.4 [Tier M, host-user SSH] Append `SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_2="<aleksandr-pubkey-from-P1.1>"` to `.env`. Double-quoted format matching existing `SSH_PUBLIC_KEY` line. Care with shell-escaping the pubkey string.
- P1.5 [Tier R, host-user SSH] Confirm via `grep ^SSH_PUBLIC_KEY .env`: BOTH lines present, key text byte-equal to P1.1 capture.
- P1.6 Report to Aen with operations-log entry. NO recreate. NO verify-keys-installed (would require recreate). Status: "ready for Phase 2 dispatch when maintenance window opens."

What I need from you to compose the dispatch:

A. The exact SSH-as-host-user command shape for apex's bare-metal host (user@host:port + key path). If `rc-connect.ps1` has a route wrapper, cite both the wrapper and the raw ssh.

B. The expected path to the apex compose dir on the bare-metal host. If you have to surface "PO must confirm", flag that as a Phase 1 pre-flight.

C. Exact P1.3 and P1.4 command text. The shell-escape risk on P1.4 is real вҖ” Aleksandr's pubkey ends with a comment like `user@host` which is fine, but if it has any quotes or special chars in the comment we have to handle it. Recommend a tee-from-stdin pattern OR a temp-file pattern over `echo X >> .env` to avoid quoting hell. Show the exact shell command Hopper would type.

D. Updated risk catalog for Phase 1 only. The recreate-related risks (live ssh session drop, volume integrity) are out of Phase 1 scope; Phase 1 has only `.env`-edit risks (malformed line вҶ’ silent ignore on next rebuild, not lockout вҖ” already in your D6).

E. Acknowledge the Phase 2 deferral: explicit note that we are NOT verifying end-to-end this round. Phase 2 will need PO sanction with maintenance window confirmation.

Also: `.env.example` follow-up (your "separate, lower-priority" flag) вҖ” keep that deferred to me. I'll amend the example file with a documented `SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_2` slot as a small Aen-side commit after Phase 1 lands successfully.

Report back with the revised Phase 1 dispatch package. Once you return it, I review and sanction, then you dispatch Hopper directly (Brunel-routed common case per Hopper's prompt). CC me on the dispatch so I have visibility. (*FR:Aen*)

text:
[2026-05-20 16:53] Brunel вҖ” overall package shape approved, Phase 2 deferral framing approved, locate-probe-as-fallback approved. Five corrections needed before I sanction. Bias: this is Hopper's FIRST dispatch ever and the operations-log entry becomes a public audit artifact вҖ” let's nail the patterns.

Q1 (compose dir from PO): I'll ask PO via UI question in parallel with this message and forward the answer. If PO has it, you bake the path in; if not, P1.2 probe stays as-is.

Q2 (Hopper extracts pubkey herself): YES, confirmed. Container-user SSH port 2222 is in her substrate scope. Pubkey is non-secret by definition. Proceed with P1.1 as Hopper-side extraction.

---

**CORRECTIONS:**

**C1 вҖ” P1.4 line-ending hazard is real; switch to base64-transit pattern.**

Your stdin-redirect pattern (`$line | ssh ... "cat >> .env"`) has two silent failure modes on PowerShell-on-Windows:
- PowerShell may emit CRLF (`\r\n`) on stdout pipes depending on `$OutputEncoding` and PSVersion. A stray CR lands in `.env`; the `SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_2` value gets a trailing `\r` byte. The entrypoint's `printf "%b"` writes the CR into `authorized_keys`; sshd silently rejects the malformed line. Aleksandr's key fails to install at Phase 2 verification вҖ” exactly the kind of "diagnosed but doesn't actually work" failure that hides until rebuild.
- TTY allocation by ssh client when stdin is a pipe is client-dependent; OpenSSH-on-Windows usually auto-disables TTY but `-T` should be explicit.

**Switch P1.4 to base64-transit pattern.** Encode the pubkey on the Windows side; reconstitute via `base64 -d` on the remote. Eliminates all PowerShell line-ending and shell-escape risk in transit. Suggested shape:

```powershell
# After P1.1 captured Aleksandr's key into $alekKey (trimmed of CR/LF/whitespace):
$alekKeyB64 = [Convert]::ToBase64String([Text.Encoding]::UTF8.GetBytes($alekKey))

# Remote command: decode b64 into a shell var, printf into .env with explicit \n
$remoteCmd = "KEY=`$(echo '$alekKeyB64' | base64 -d); printf 'SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_2=\`"%s\`"\n' `"`$KEY`" >> '$COMPOSE_DIR/.env'"
ssh -T dev@100.96.54.170 $remoteCmd
```

(Refine the exact PowerShell quoting as needed вҖ” the principle is: pubkey crosses the wire as a single base64 token, reconstituted server-side, never word-split or expanded by any shell on the way.) Add `ssh -T` explicitly. If you prefer a tempfile-based alternative (write line to local tempfile with explicit LF, scp to remote, ssh-cat-append), document that as the fallback.

**C2 вҖ” P1.1 needs an explicit capture pattern, not "Hopper figures it out."**

The dispatch must give Hopper the exact PowerShell pipeline that:
1. SSHes to container, cats `authorized_keys`
2. Filters out PO's line (by `michelek` comment match)
3. Strips trailing CR/LF/whitespace
4. Assigns to `$alekKey`
5. Verifies exactly ONE line remained (defensive)

Suggested:
```powershell
$allKeys = ssh -i $env:USERPROFILE\.ssh\id_ed25519_apex -p 2222 ai-teams@100.96.54.170 'cat /home/ai-teams/.ssh/authorized_keys'
$nonPoLines = @($allKeys | Where-Object { $_ -notmatch 'michelek\s*$' -and $_.Trim() -ne '' })
if ($nonPoLines.Count -ne 1) {
    Write-Host "[SURFACE-BACK] Expected exactly 1 non-PO key, found $($nonPoLines.Count). Surface to Brunel."
    # halt dispatch
}
$alekKey = $nonPoLines[0].TrimEnd("`r","`n"," ","`t")
```

The `@()` force-array idiom prevents the count-of-1-becomes-string trap. The Trim eliminates the line-ending issue at the source.

**C3 вҖ” SSH -T flag for any stdin-redirect or non-interactive SSH invocation.**

Add `-T` to every `ssh dev@100.96.54.170 ...` invocation in P1.3, P1.4, P1.5, P1.5b that doesn't allocate a TTY by design. Explicit-no-TTY is safer than relying on client auto-detect. Container-user SSH in P1.1 can keep current shape since it's plain cat output.

**C4 вҖ” Backup filename HHMM is hardcoded; make it dynamic.**

P1.3 says "if 2026-05-20 backup already exists, append -1648 (HHMM)". The 1648 is a stale literal from earlier in our exchange. Make it: collision-check first, then if collision append actual current HHMM:

```powershell
$bakBase = ".env.bak.2026-05-20"
$now = Get-Date -Format "HHmm"
$bakName = $bakBase
# remote check
$exists = ssh -T dev@100.96.54.170 "test -f $COMPOSE_DIR/$bakBase && echo EXISTS || echo OK"
if ($exists -eq "EXISTS") { $bakName = "$bakBase-$now" }
ssh -T dev@100.96.54.170 "cd $COMPOSE_DIR && cp .env '$bakName' && ls -la .env '$bakName'"
```

Or remote-side equivalent in one shot. Whatever's cleanest in your judgment вҖ” just stop hardcoding 1648.

**C5 вҖ” P1.5 byte-equality check needs an explicit method.**

You said "Compare _2 line's value byte-for-byte against the P1.1 capture." How? Show the exact compare command. Suggestion:

```powershell
$envLine = ssh -T dev@100.96.54.170 "grep '^SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_2=' $COMPOSE_DIR/.env"
# Extract value between the double-quotes
$envValue = $envLine -replace '^SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_2="(.+)"$', '$1'
if ($envValue -ne $alekKey) {
    Write-Host "[SURFACE-BACK] .env _2 value mismatch from P1.1 capture. Pre-Phase-2 fail. Restore from backup."
    # surface
}
```

This catches both "transit corrupted the value" and "Hopper appended to wrong file" failure modes.

---

**OK on the rest as-written:**

- P1.2 locate-probe вҖ” fine; let PO short-circuit if she has the path.
- P1.5b `docker compose config` Tier R + rollback path on parse error вҖ” good catch, keep.
- Phase 2 deferral framing вҖ” good as-is.
- Risk catalog вҖ” covers Phase 1 surface; folds in the new transit-corruption risk that C1+C2 now mitigate.
- `.env.example` follow-up deferred to me вҖ” confirmed.

---

**Composition order:**

1. You revise dispatch text with C1вҖ“C5 folded in. Wait for my forwarded compose-dir answer from PO.
2. Once compose dir is in (or P1.2 probe confirmed as fallback), you send me the FINAL dispatch text. I sanction explicitly with one line ("Aen sanction: M-acks at P1.3 and P1.4 confirmed; dispatch ratified вҖ” proceed to Hopper").
3. You SendMessage to Hopper with the dispatch verbatim. CC me.
4. Hopper executes; reports to you (closes diagnosis loop) + CCs me (team-level role-of-record).
5. After Hopper's report, I commit the operations log + amend `.env.example` (separate work).

Standing by for your revised package. (*FR:Aen*)
summary: 5 corrections to Phase-1 package; switch to base64

в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
DEPLOYED-ARTIFACTS READ (your discipline; done before ack)

Done. Confirmed your diagnosis:
- entrypoint-apex.sh вҖ” Step 7 header at line 166, env-var enumeration loop at 169-177, install-for-both-users loop at 179-189, sshd start at 191-193, else-warning at 194-196. Multi-key support is fully entrypoint-wired: `for var in $(env | grep '^SSH_PUBLIC_KEY' | sort | cut -d= -f1)` reads every matching var, builds `$KEYS` with `\n` between, then `printf "%b" "$KEYS"` writes for BOTH `/home/michelek` AND `/home/ai-teams` (good вҖ” Aleksandr's key lands on the ai-teams account he uses).
- docker-compose.yml вҖ” both `SSH_PUBLIC_KEY=${SSH_PUBLIC_KEY:-}` and `SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_2=${SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_2:-}` at lines 53-54. Default-empty fallback (`:-`) means unset .env var вҶ’ empty string вҶ’ Step 7's `[ -n "$val" ]` skips. Adding .env line is drop-in; no compose-yml edit needed.
- .env.example:13 вҖ” template ships only SSH_PUBLIC_KEY slot, no _2.

CITATION NOTE for ops-log: you cited entrypoint-apex.sh:169-189; my truthful read range covering the full Step 7 block is 166-196. I'll cite 166-196 in the deployed-artifacts-read declaration (the audit surface needs what I actually read, not what was quoted). The functional code you referenced is wholly within my range вҖ” no semantic discrepancy. Flagging for transparency, not surfacing-back.

в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
TIER VALIDATION AGAINST DEPLOYED-ARTIFACTS

P1.5b вҖ” `docker compose config` вҖ” confirmed Tier R. The compose CLI's `config` subcommand renders the interpolated YAML to stdout; does not invoke the service lifecycle. No start/stop/restart/recreate. Validates as Tier R.

All other tier classifications match my read. No re-classification needed. No Tier D in dispatch as you stated.

в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
NUMBERED UNDERSTANDING (per my "How You Work" Step 2)

I understand the dispatch to require, in strict sequence with hard-gate-surface-back between each:

1. **P1.1** [Tier R, container-user SSH port 2222] Extract Aleksandr's pubkey from /home/ai-teams/.ssh/authorized_keys in the live apex container. Filter out PO's line (regex `michelek\s*$`) and blank lines using @() force-array idiom. Pass criterion: exactly 1 non-PO non-empty line, $nonPoLines.Count -eq 1. TrimEnd $alekKey of CR/LF/space/tab. Surface-back if 0 (diagnosis wrong) or 2+ (unexpected state).

2. **P1.2** [Tier R, host-user SSH] Locate apex compose dir via three-fallback ls + find ~/ -maxdepth 4 -name docker-compose.yml -path "*apex*". Pass criterion: exactly one match containing docker-compose.yml + .env; capture as $COMPOSE_DIR. Surface-back on 0 or 2+ matches. Then ls -la $COMPOSE_DIR/.env to confirm dev-owned/writable; surface-back if not.

3. **P1.3** [Tier M, your single-line ack quoted in primary dispatch] Backup .env to $bakBase=".env.bak.2026-05-20" with collision-handling: if file exists, append -HHMM suffix. cp .env $bakName; ls -la both files. Pass criterion: identical sizes, backup timestamp newer. Capture $bakName for ops-log + Phase-2 rollback reference.

4. **P1.4** [Tier M, your single-line ack quoted in primary dispatch] Append SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_2 line via base64-transit pattern: $alekKeyB64 = [Convert]::ToBase64String([Text.Encoding]::UTF8.GetBytes($alekKey)); remote command decodes via `KEY=$(echo '<b64>' | base64 -d); printf 'SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_2="%s"\n' "$KEY" >> '$COMPOSE_DIR/.env'`. Print $remoteCmd before ssh invocation for sanity-check. Pass criterion: ssh exit 0, no stderr. Fallback (scp-tempfile-LF) documented but NOT switched-to without surfacing-back to you first.

5. **P1.5** [Tier R] Verify .env state. grep `^SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_2=` for the new line; regex-strip the quotes to get $envValue; compare byte-equal to $alekKey. Then grep -c `^SSH_PUBLIC_KEY` for total count == 2. On either mismatch: cp $bakName .env to restore, then surface-back with hex-dump of $alekKey + hex-dump of $envValue + diff. Catches the silent-transit-corruption failure mode and the wrong-file-append failure mode before they propagate to Phase 2.

6. **P1.5b** [Tier R] cd $COMPOSE_DIR && docker compose config 2>&1 | grep SSH_PUBLIC_KEY. Pass criterion: rendered compose output includes both SSH_PUBLIC_KEY=... and SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_2=... with resolved values; zero stderr; ssh exit 0. Read-only вҖ” does NOT start/stop/restart/recreate the running container. в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
PROBE EXECUTION

I built the docker-inspect command via base64-transit (your P1.4 pattern) to dodge a local-side ssh-passthrough quoting issue. Remote literal after decode:

  docker inspect apex-research --format '{{ index .Config.Labels "com.docker.compose.project_working_dir" }}' 2>&1

ssh exit: 0. Output: empty string. By your Failure-mode taxonomy: "Empty output (label not present, container not running, command failed) вҶ’ surface back."

в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
WITHIN-DISPATCH-AGENCY DIAGNOSTIC (Tier R, confirming substrate-readiness)

I ran one Tier R confirmatory probe вҖ” dump ALL labels as JSON вҖ” to identify whether the empty was due to (a) container not running, (b) label key wrong, or (c) command malformed. The JSON-format query returned:

  {
    "com.docker.compose.config-hash": "ad74af459ed8bec4b5d466f39a46e40c86115aab145cc10f732737cca274b539",
    "com.docker.compose.container-number": "1",

    "com.docker.compose.oneoff": "False",
    "com.docker.compose.project": "apex-migration-research",
    "com.docker.compose.project.config_files": "/home/dev/github/apex-migration-research/docker-compose.yml",
    "com.docker.compose.project.working_dir": "/home/dev/github/apex-migration-research",
    "com.docker.compose.service": "apex-research",

    "org.opencontainers.image.version": "24.04"
  }

в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
ROOT CAUSE OF THE EMPTY

The actual label key is `com.docker.compose.project.working_dir` (DOTS between `project` and `working_dir`). The dispatched probe asked for `com.docker.compose.project_working_dir` (UNDERSCORE). `index` lookup on a missing key returns empty вҖ” substrate did exactly what was asked; the ask was slightly off-spec.

For reference: docker compose v2.x uses dot-separated label namespace (`project.working_dir`, `project.config_files`). The underscore form was the v1 convention. The container's compose version label is `5.1.0` which is well into the v2 era.

в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
WHAT THE JSON DUMP CONFIRMS (substrate-truth, NOT my own diagnostic patching)

- `com.docker.compose.project.working_dir` = `/home/dev/github/apex-migration-research` вҖ” matches HIT 1.
- `com.docker.compose.project.config_files` = `/home/dev/github/apex-migration-research/docker-compose.yml` вҖ” reinforces HIT 1.
- Container is running (the JSON returned).
- The `.pre-fresh-clone-2026-04-29` dir (HIT 2) is NOT referenced by the running container.

в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
WHAT I'M NOT DOING

- NOT silently switching the label-key from underscore to dot-form and re-running. The dispatch was specific; the substrate disagreed; surface-back is your call to make.
- NOT proceeding to P1.3 even though the JSON dump unambiguously identifies HIT 1.

в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
RE-DISPATCH OPTIONS

Option B' вҖ” re-dispatch with the corrected label key `com.docker.compose.project.working_dir` (dots). Cheap; substrate-truth still drives the answer.

Option A (now better-evidenced) вҖ” accept HIT 1 = /home/dev/github/apex-migration-research as canonical $COMPOSE_DIR on the strength of the JSON labels dump (which is substrate-truth, not naming-convention inference). Faster path; the diagnostic dump effectively did the work of the dispatched probe.

Option C вҖ” verify with PO before committing $COMPOSE_DIR.

I'd take Option A given the JSON dump answers the question authoritatively, but it's your call.

Tier R only; no mutations occurred. Standing by. вҖ” Hopper

text:
[2026-05-20 17:23] Brunel вҖ” your 17:19 dot-form correction crossed my 17:21 surface-back in transit. I re-ran with the corrected key. (*FR:Hopper*)

в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
P1.2a вҖ” DISAMBIG CLEARED

Corrected probe (dot-form `com.docker.compose.project.working_dir`) returned:

  /home/dev/github/apex-migration-research

Matches HIT 1 exactly. `$COMPOSE_DIR` set canonically; ssh exit 0. Saved to local tempfile for downstream steps.

(Sanity check: my 17:21 JSON-labels dump diagnostic showed the same value under the same key вҖ” substrate-truth is consistent both via direct lookup and via labels-dump.)

в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
P1.2b вҖ” NEW HARD-GATE: .env NOT FOUND

Probe:
  ssh -T dev@100.96.54.170 "ls -la '/home/dev/github/apex-migration-research/.env' 2>&1; echo WHOAMI; whoami; id"

Output:
  ls: cannot access '/home/dev/github/apex-migration-research/.env': No such file or directory
  WHOAMI
  dev
  uid=1000(dev) gid=1000(dev) groups=1000(dev),24(cdrom),25(floppy),27(sudo),29(audio),30(dip),44(video),46(plugdev),100(users),101(netdev),102(scanner),106(bluetooth),108(lpadmin),985(docker),989(ollama)

ssh exit 0. No file. Host-user is `dev`, sudo-capable, in `docker` group вҖ” substrate-access shape is fine; the .env file just isn't where the dispatch assumed.

в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
SUBSTRATE-DISAGREEMENT WITH DISPATCH PREMISE

The r3 dispatch (background section) said: "host-side .env on the RC bare-metal host only sets SSH_PUBLIC_KEY." Implicit premise: .env file exists at the compose dir. The compose label `config_files=/home/dev/github/apex-migration-research/docker-compose.yml` confirms the compose file is there, but there is no sibling .env.

Possible explanations (do NOT proceed-probe on my own initiative вҖ” your call):
1. .env lives elsewhere (different filename, different path, --env-file flag to compose at startup, etc.)
2. Compose project does not use a .env at all; SSH_PUBLIC_KEY is injected via shell environment / systemd unit / wrapper script
3. .env was consumed and removed at startup (unusual)

в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
WHAT I'M NOT DOING

- NOT running `find $COMPOSE_DIR -name '.env*'` or `find / -name '.env' -path "*apex*"` on my own initiative вҖ” that is scope-expansion-probe past the hard-gate.
- NOT inspecting how the live container was launched (e.g., `docker inspect --format ...Config.Env`) to discover where SSH_PUBLIC_KEY came from вҖ” same reason.
- NOT proceeding to P1.3. The whole Phase-1 fix-shape (append SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_2 to .env) presupposes .env exists. The premise is wrong; the plan needs to be re-shaped, not patched.

в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
RE-DISPATCH OPTIONS (your call)

Option A вҖ” sanction a directed probe to find the actual env-source. Suggested shapes:
  i.  `ls -la /home/dev/github/apex-migration-research/` (full directory listing вҖ” see what config files are actually there)
  ii. `docker inspect apex-research --format "{{range .Config.Env}}{{println .}}{{end}}" | grep SSH_PUBLIC_KEY` (read the container's effective env to see what SSH_PUBLIC_KEY resolved to, and reverse-engineer where it came from)
  iii. `cat /home/dev/github/apex-migration-research/docker-compose.yml | grep -A2 env_file` (see if compose declares a non-default env_file path)

SYNTHESIS (descriptive вҖ” not patching the plan, not proceeding)

Joining the three probes:

A. **Compose currently resolves all SSH_PUBLIC_KEY* slots to empty.** A fresh `docker compose up --force-recreate` today would yield a container with NO ssh keys at all вҖ” breaking access for both PO and Aleksandr.

B. **The running container's Config.Env shows PO's key in SLOT 2.** That value came from somewhere. Given (A), the running container's Config.Env was resolved at an earlier point in time, before whatever event emptied the .env. The container has been running since.

C. **The 2026-04-29 fresh-clone event likely wiped the operational `.env`.** The compose-dir was re-cloned on that date (the suffix on the backup dir literally says so). A fresh clone of the repo would not include `.env` (it's gitignored by convention). The pre-fresh-clone backup at `apex-migration-research.pre-fresh-clone-2026-04-29/.env` is plausibly the LAST operational .env, frozen at the moment of the fresh-clone.

D. **The SLOT 2 mystery.** Why was PO's key in `SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_2` and not `SSH_PUBLIC_KEY`? Either (i) the original operational .env had PO's key under the `_2` key (intentional, perhaps to leave slot-1 reserved for something), or (ii) someone hand-edited the running container's env after create вҖ” but env vars on a running container are immutable in Docker, so (i) is the only plausible explanation.

E. **There is no `env_file:` directive** in compose. Compose's default behavior is to read `.env` from the project working directory. With no `.env` there, all variables fall through to the `${VAR:-}` defaults (empty).

F. **vjs_apex_apps/.env exists in a SIBLING repo** but the apex-research compose-yml resolves $COMPOSE_DIR-relative; that .env is unrelated unless someone has been doing weird path tricks.

HYPOTHESIS RANK (your H1-H4 framing)

- **H1 (env-file at non-default path):** REJECTED. Probe 2 confirms no `env_file:` directive. Compose is looking at default-path `.env` which doesn't exist.
- **H2 (shell-env-injection on `docker compose up`):** PLAUSIBLE but requires you-side investigation. PO could be running `SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_2="..." docker compose up` from a wrapper script or `.bashrc` export. Probe-4 territory.
- **H3 (env_file in compose-yml):** REJECTED. Same as H1.
- **H4 (a layer we haven't seen):** PARTIAL. The simplest answer is **H5 (new): the container is a stable-state survivor from before the 2026-04-29 fresh-clone**. The `.env` that produced its current Config.Env still exists at `apex-migration-research.pre-fresh-clone-2026-04-29/.env` вҖ” and the Phase-1 fix might need to repair the operational compose dir BEFORE a future Phase-2 recreate, by restoring `.env` from the backup dir.

в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
WHAT I'M NOT DOING

- NOT cat-ing the backup-dir's `.env` to confirm it contains PO's key in slot 2. That would be Tier R but it's a scope expansion past your dispatch.

SUB-SHAPE E вҖ” INTERNALIZED

Your phrasing is sharper than mine. Restating to confirm I understand:

The substrate has THREE candidate truth-sources at different layers:
1. **FR design-as-shipped** (`designs/deployed/<team>/container/*` in this repo) вҖ” canonical for design lineage.
2. **Consumer team operational copy** (e.g., `/home/dev/github/apex-migration-research/`) вҖ” canonical for what compose actually reads at next `docker compose up`.
3. **Running container state** (Config.Env, mounted volumes, in-process state) вҖ” canonical for what's serving traffic right now.

Each layer can drift from its neighbor. The operator's premise-validation discipline must distinguish:
- Read FR design вҶ’ catches design-vs-design-intent drift
- Read consumer operational copy вҶ’ catches design-vs-operational drift (the gap that bit P1.2b)
- Probe running container вҶ’ catches operational-vs-runtime drift (the gap that bit P1.2c slot-assignment)

My existing "read deployed-artifacts before executing" discipline (per `hopper.md` Diagnostic Discipline section) reads layer 1 only. That is INSUFFICIENT for FR-shipped substrates the consumer team operationalizes. For first-dispatch against a substrate I have not seen, the operational-copy read needs to happen too вҖ” probe-via-substrate, not file-read-via-this-repo.

If we Cal-Protocol-A this together, the pattern shape I'd draft:

> **Substrate-truth has three layers when FR ships design and consumer teams operationalize:** design-as-shipped (FR's repo), operational-on-substrate-host (consumer team's compose dir on the bare-metal host), and runtime-in-container (Config.Env, live process state). Each layer can drift from its neighbor without notice. An operator's pre-execution discipline that reads only the FR design layer can ship past consumer-team-amendments and runtime-baked-state. **Defense: when first-dispatching against a substrate, run a Tier R probe-suite that surfaces all three layers and reconcile before committing to a fix-shape.**

If the framing tracks, that's the joint-authorship piece вҖ” yours is the architectural distinction, mine is the operator-side defense pattern.

LOAD-BEARING CONTRADICTIONS vs P1.2c FINDINGS (factual observations, NOT proposing mutations)

Cross-walking this backup against P1.2c Probe 1 output (running container's Config.Env):

**Contradiction 1 вҖ” SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_2 in container, NOT in backup.**
- Running container Config.Env (P1.2c Probe 1): `SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_2=<PO's key>`, `SSH_PUBLIC_KEY=` (empty).
- Backup .env: `SSH_PUBLIC_KEY="<PO's key>"` (slot 1, value-quoted), NO `SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_2` entry at all.

This means the running container was NOT created from this backup. Either:
(a) Container predates this backup (container was last (re)created before 2026-04-29 17:16, and the operational .env at that time had PO's key in slot 2, value-quoted-or-not. Then someone migrated PO's key from slot 2 вҶ’ slot 1 in the .env at some point, took a backup at 2026-04-29 17:16, then deleted the operational .env via fresh-clone), OR
(b) Container was created from a different .env that we haven't found yet (shell-env injection variant, or a deleted intermediate .env), OR
(c) Backup was taken at a different timepoint than what the suffix suggests, and timeline reconstruction is more complex.

**Contradiction 2 вҖ” SLOT 3 was pre-existing.**
- Backup has `SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_3=ssh-ed25519 ...JjcNpgo... rc-connect`. That value is value-key for a key tagged `rc-connect`.
- Probe 2 (rendered compose config) showed `SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_3: ""` as a wired-but-empty slot.

So compose-yml had SLOT 3 BEFORE the 2026-04-29 fresh-clone. My deployed-artifacts read at session start showed only SLOTS 1+2 at `docker-compose.yml:53-54` вҖ” that's FR-design that's stale relative to the operational copy on the consumer team's host. Sub-shape E manifesting directly: FR-design (2 slots) drifted from operational-on-substrate (3 slots) drifted from runtime-in-container (Config.Env has SLOT 2 populated, SLOT 1 empty, SLOT 3 absent).

The `rc-connect` comment is suggestive вҖ” it implies the SLOT 3 key was for an rc-connect wrapper to reach the container. Probably the apex team added this when adopting rc-connect tooling.

**Contradiction 3 вҖ” full credentials cluster in backup that aren't in container probe output (because P1.2c Probe 1 grepped only SSH_PUBLIC_KEY*).**
- Backup has GITHUB_TOKEN, ATLASSIAN_API_TOKEN, ATLASSIAN_EMAIL, ATLASSIAN_BASE_URL, TUNNEL_TOKEN.
- We haven't probed Container Config.Env for any of these. If the running container has them too (likely; otherwise things like the jira-mcp-server wouldn't work), they were baked in at create-time same as the SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_2.

в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
ANSWER-MAP FOR PO Q's (per your 17:33 framing)

- **Q1 (why was .env not restored post-2026-04-29 fresh-clone):** STILL FOR PO. The backup exists; the operational dir doesn't have it. Some intentional or accidental gap.
- **Q2 (what's SLOT 3 for):** PARTIALLY ANSWERED. Backup shows `SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_3=...rc-connect` вҖ” the slot was reserved for the rc-connect wrapper's pubkey. PO should still confirm whether this is still operational.

**How it happened:** your r3 dispatch cited `rc-deployments.json` entries `num:"1"` (host SSH) and `num:"2"` (container SSH 2222) вҖ” these were the entries that fit the apex-substrate pattern by inspection, but you didn't verify they were the canonical PRODUCTION host. Hopper saw the full registry at spawn (her intro confirms reading num:1 and num:2), would have also seen PROD-LLM at num:9 but the dispatch text named the host explicitly вҖ” her discipline correctly followed the dispatch.

**Implications:**

1. ALL the "degraded substrate" findings apply to RC-server (100.96.54.170), NOT to your actual production apex (PROD-LLM).

**Sub-shape F added to the pattern catalog:** **registry-entry-choice-from-first-match** вҖ” picking the first registry entry that looks like the target without confirming canonical operational deployment. Same generalization (substrate-truth vs offline-inference) at the substrate-selection layer. Belongs alongside A-E.

**Close-out artifact updates needed (relay to Hopper before she commits her ops-log + scratchpad):**

A. **Hopper's ops-log P1.6 entry** must lead with this correction. Dispatch summary should read: "...ABORTED MID-EXECUTION. Diagnostic operated against RC-server (100.96.54.170), confirmed post-execution to be a non-production substrate; canonical production apex is PROD-LLM (10.100.136.162, rc-deployments.json num:9), untouched. E. **Phase-1 fix-shape for PROD-LLM** вҖ” entirely new. Whatever we eventually compose against PROD-LLM is a fresh dispatch in a future session. The RC-server findings inform NONE of the PROD-LLM diagnostic except by analogy.

[2026-05-20 19:13] [SURFACE-BACK] P3.6 hard-gate tripped. Halted at remote-side `source` failure. Substrate unchanged (no .env written; verified by post-fail probe). (*FR:Hopper*)

в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
WHAT HAPPENED

P3.1-P3.5 all PASSED (substrate state unchanged, all four values captured: $poKey 117 chars, $alekKey 114 chars, $rcKey 91 chars, $ghToken 40 chars starts `gho_`).

P3.6 ssh round-trip executed. Remote bash hit `set -e` (from `set -euo pipefail`) and exited 127 at line:
  `set -a; source '/home/dev/github/apex-migration-research.pre-fresh-clone-2026-04-29/.env'; set +a`

Stderr:
```
/home/dev/github/apex-migration-research.pre-fresh-clone-2026-04-29/.env: line 11: AAAAC3NzaC1lZDI1NTE5AAAAIO2fgnCIWJjcNpgo/rjGmF5e0fr35qupLHAFk57qU6tB: No such file or directory
```

в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
ROOT CAUSE (substrate-disagrees-with-dispatch-premise)

Looking at the backup `.env` line 11 from P1.2d + P2.2 captures:

  `SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_3=ssh-ed25519 AAAAC3NzaC1lZDI1NTE5AAAAIO2fgnCIWJjcNpgo/rjGmF5e0fr35qupLHAFk57qU6tB rc-connect`

The value is **unquoted** and contains spaces. The other SSH_PUBLIC_KEY entries in the backup .env ARE double-quoted (`SSH_PUBLIC_KEY="ssh-ed25519 AAAA... apex-research"`), but SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_3 is not.

When bash's `source` reads this with `set -a`, it parses `SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_3=ssh-ed25519` as the env assignment, then tries to execute `AAAAC3NzaC1lZDI1NTE5...` as the next command on the line. `set -e` then halts the script. The `cat > .env <<EOF` block never executed.

в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
SUBSTRATE STATE вҖ” VERIFIED UNCHANGED

I ran a Tier R post-fail probe (`ls -la $COMPOSE_DIR/.env 2>&1; ls $COMPOSE_DIR/`) to confirm nothing partial got written. Result:

  `ls: cannot access '/home/dev/github/apex-migration-research/.env': No such file or directory`

Substrate is in pre-P3 state. **No rollback needed** (rm would no-op anyway). Backup .env untouched. Container running on baked-in Config.Env as before.

в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
WHAT I'M NOT DOING

- NOT silently patching the dispatch by switching to a different compose strategy (e.g., grep-and-build instead of source-and-cat). The dispatch's `source $BACKUP_DIR/.env` is explicit; replacing it is a dispatch-edit, your call not mine.
- NOT running `rm -f $COMPOSE_DIR/.env` вҖ” no `.env` exists; rollback is a no-op; substrate is already in pre-P3 state.
- NOT proceeding to P3.7. P3.6 pass-criterion not met.

в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
RE-DISPATCH OPTIONS (your call)

**Option A вҖ” quote-then-source workaround.** Pre-process backup .env server-side to quote the SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_3 value, source the patched copy from `/tmp`:

```bash
# Pre-process: add quotes to SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_3 value
sed 's|^SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_3=\(.*\)$|SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_3="\1"|' '$BACKUP_DIR/.env' > /tmp/apex-env-patched
set -a
source /tmp/apex-env-patched
set +a
rm /tmp/apex-env-patched
```

Simplest patch; preserves the dispatch's overall sourceless+expansion flow.

**Option B вҖ” drop `source`; grep-extract each token individually.** Same pattern as P3.3-P3.5 for individual values:

```bash
GITHUB_TOKEN_VAL=$(grep '^GITHUB_TOKEN=' '$BACKUP_DIR/.env' | cut -d= -f2- | sed 's/^"//;s/"$//')
# ... same for ATLASSIAN_EMAIL, ATLASSIAN_API_TOKEN, ATLASSIAN_BASE_URL, TUNNEL_TOKEN, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
```

Verbose but no `source` involved; each value gets explicit unquoting. More substrate-truthful (matches what we already did for pubkeys); doesn't rely on the backup .env being syntactically valid bash.

**Option C вҖ” quote the backup file itself.** Tier M write to `$BACKUP_DIR/.env` to add quotes around SLOT 3. **Probably wrong** вҖ” that mutates the rollback artifact, which we want to preserve untouched.

**Option D вҖ” fix the heredoc target's SLOT 3 expansion server-side.** Since we already have `$RC_KEY` captured separately via P3.4 (independently of `source`), we don't actually need SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_3 from the sourced env. The fix is to skip `source`-ing SLOT 3, source everything else, then use the P3.4-captured value. But the source-statement itself will still trip over SLOT 3 even if we don't use the value вҖ” so this needs A or B's pre-process or full-extract approach anyway.

I lean toward **Option B** вҖ” it's the most substrate-truthful (no source-dependency, each value extracted explicitly), follows the same shape we already use for pubkeys, and doesn't require pre-processing the backup. Option A is the smallest patch. Your call.

text:
[2026-05-20 19:16] Brunel вҖ” wake. Hopper surface-back at 19:13 in your inbox; she's re-pinged three times (19:15/19:15/19:16) with no autonomous wake on your side. Process now.

**Headline:** P3.6 hard-gate tripped. Backup `.env` has `SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_3` unquoted; bash `source` fails to parse it (pubkey has spaces; unquoted = word-split). Visible in P2 diff in retrospect; we didn't anticipate the `source` interaction. P3.1-P3.5 all PASSED; pubkey + GH_TOKEN values captured in session TEMP and valid. No mutation occurred; substrate unchanged. Tier R only so far.

**Her surfaced options:**
- A: sed-patched copy of backup `.env` in /tmp, then source the patched copy (avoids mutating rollback artifact)
- **B: drop `source`; grep-extract each token individually** (same pattern as pubkey extraction in P3.3-P3.4)
- C: quote the backup `.env` in place вҖ” REJECTED (mutates rollback artifact)
- D: variant of A

**My read: Option B.** Most substrate-truthful, no /tmp ceremony, pattern consistency with the working P3.3/P3.4 extraction. Costs are minimal: 5 additional `ssh -T ... grep | cut` round-trips instead of one `source`. Each one is Tier R, default-permitted; no new sanction required beyond what's already in P3.6's Brunel ack (which covered "compose new `.env` on host", not specifically the `source` mechanism).

**Amend P3.6 with Option B implementation:**

```bash
# Instead of `source backup-env`, grep-extract each token:
GITHUB_TOKEN_VAL="$(grep '^GITHUB_TOKEN=' $BACKUP_DIR/.env | cut -d= -f2- | tr -d '"')"
ATLASSIAN_EMAIL_VAL="$(grep '^ATLASSIAN_EMAIL=' $BACKUP_DIR/.env | cut -d= -f2- | tr -d '"')"
ATLASSIAN_API_TOKEN_VAL="$(grep '^ATLASSIAN_API_TOKEN=' $BACKUP_DIR/.env | cut -d= -f2- | tr -d '"')"
ATLASSIAN_BASE_URL_VAL="$(grep '^ATLASSIAN_BASE_URL=' $BACKUP_DIR/.env | cut -d= -f2- | tr -d '"')"
TUNNEL_TOKEN_VAL="$(grep '^TUNNEL_TOKEN=' $BACKUP_DIR/.env | cut -d= -f2- | tr -d '"')"
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY_VAL="$(grep '^ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=' $BACKUP_DIR/.env | cut -d= -f2- | tr -d '"')"
```

Then the `cat > .env <<EOF` block uses `"$GITHUB_TOKEN_VAL"` etc. instead of `"$GITHUB_TOKEN"` (the sourced var).

`tr -d '"'` strips the surrounding double-quotes the backup uses on quoted values. ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is empty in backup вҶ’ grep returns empty string вҶ’ that's the right behavior. TUNNEL_TOKEN was populated in backup (per P2 diff).

Dispatch amendment-1 to Hopper. Sanction still inherits from your original P3.6 Brunel ack ("Tier M, write new `.env`, append-only on the substrate, rollback by rm"). Don't re-author the ack; just specify the new extraction mechanism.

Process surface-back now. (*FR:Aen*)

[2026-05-20 19:27] Brunel вҖ” wake. Phase 1 success ack from Hopper at 19:23. Before relaying Phase 2, HOLD on Hopper's flag:

**Her flag:** "GH_TOKEN + TUNNEL_TOKEN in `.env` but not in compose-yml `environment:` block; not blocking but worth knowing for next recreate."

**Material concern for Phase 2:** if the operational compose-yml's `environment:` block doesn't enumerate `GH_TOKEN` and `TUNNEL_TOKEN`, docker compose will read `.env` for variable substitution but NOT pass those vars into the container's Config.Env at recreate. After Phase 2:
- Authorized_keys would still get all 3 SSH keys (entrypoint reads `SSH_PUBLIC_KEY*` from Config.Env) вң“
- Container's Config.Env would have `SSH_PUBLIC_KEY*` populated вң“
- But `GH_TOKEN` and `TUNNEL_TOKEN` would be ABSENT from Config.Env even though they're in `.env`
- Result: gh CLI broken, cloudflared tunnel broken on next recreate

Sub-shape E rears its head again вҖ” operational compose-yml on apex's host may have an enumerated `environment:` block that differs from FR's design copy. We already know SLOT 3 was added operationally; SLOT 3 added without corresponding `GH_TOKEN`/`TUNNEL_TOKEN` lines being added would explain the current Config.Env (PO key SLOT 2 + some tokens) being inconsistent with the deployed-as-shipped compose-yml.

**Recommend: Pre-Phase-2 verification probe (Tier R), then decide.**

Dispatch Hopper a quick Tier R probe before relaying Phase 2:

```
ssh -T dev@100.96.54.170 "cd '/home/dev/github/apex-migration-research' && grep -A20 'environment:' docker-compose.yml | head -30"
```

Captures the full `environment:` block of the operational compose-yml. Then we know:
- If GH_TOKEN + TUNNEL_TOKEN are listed вҶ’ Phase 2 proceeds as drafted; recreate produces complete Config.Env
- If they're missing вҶ’ either (a) amend operational compose-yml first (Tier M, adds new sanction surface, blocks Phase 2 until done) OR (b) accept Phase 2 produces partial-credential recreate, accept GH/TUNNEL break post-recreate, handle as separate fix

This is the kind of pre-Phase-2 substrate-truth check that should have been in the original Phase 2 dispatch's preconditions section. Filing as a session-wrap discipline gap on my side вҖ” pre-Tier-D dispatches should always include a `docker compose config` rendered-output check against the dispatch's expected outcome.

**Decide which (a)/(b)/other after the probe returns; don't relay Phase 2 until you and I agree on the path.**

Process the surface-back + dispatch the verification probe. CC me on the probe; I'll wait for your assessment of the result before sanctioning Phase 2 relay. (*FR:Aen*)

в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
RAW OUTPUT вҖ” apex-research service's environment block (verbatim)

```
    environment:
      - HOME=/home/ai-teams
      - REPO_URL=${REPO_URL:-https://github.com/Eesti-Raudtee/apex-migration-research.git}
      - SOURCE_REPO_URL=${SOURCE_REPO_URL:-https://github.com/Eesti-Raudtee/vjs_apex_apps.git}
      - GITHUB_TOKEN=${GITHUB_TOKEN}

      - SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_2=${SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_2:-}

      - NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS=/opt/warp-ca.pem

    # No ports: section needed вҖ” network_mode: host exposes ports directly.

    # Dashboard on port 5173.

```

`head -40` cut the output before reaching the sibling `cloudflared` service's environment block (the `restart: unless-stopped` is the end of apex-research's service stanza; cloudflared comes after but past the head cutoff). The captured 14 vars ARE the complete `environment:` for apex-research.

в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
WHAT'S DECLARED + WHAT'S MISSING

**Declared (14 vars):** HOME, REPO_URL, SOURCE_REPO_URL, GITHUB_TOKEN, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, SSH_PUBLIC_KEY, SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_2, SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_3, NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS, CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS, TEAM_NAME, ATLASSIAN_EMAIL, ATLASSIAN_API_TOKEN, ATLASSIAN_BASE_URL.

**NOT declared (relative to our `.env`):** **GH_TOKEN**, TUNNEL_TOKEN.

- **GH_TOKEN absent** вҖ” Phase 2 recreate would land a container without GH_TOKEN in Config.Env. The `gh` CLI inside apex-research falls back to `GITHUB_TOKEN` when `GH_TOKEN` is unset (both are recognized credential sources), so existing `gh auth` continues to work via `GITHUB_TOKEN` (which IS declared and IS in .env). **Practical impact of the absence is likely zero.** But: divergence from the running container's current Config.Env (which has both per P3.5 capture).
- **TUNNEL_TOKEN absent** вҖ” matches your pre-probe analysis: belongs to the sibling cloudflared service's environment block. Not apex-research's domain. Phase 2 only recreates apex-research per the standing scope, so TUNNEL_TOKEN absence is not a Phase 2 risk.

в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
TWO NOTABLE OBSERVATIONS

1. **SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_3 IS declared in operational compose-yml** (line `- SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_3=${SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_3:-}`). The 3-slot pattern is fully wired operational-side. Phase 2 recreate against our new `.env` will install all 3 SSH keys correctly вҖ” pre-condition for the recreate's success is met.

2. **GITHUB_TOKEN's interpolation differs from other tokens.** Line `- GITHUB_TOKEN=${GITHUB_TOKEN}` is the only one WITHOUT a `:-` empty-string fallback default. Compare to `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=${ANTHROPIC_API_KEY:-}` and others. If `GITHUB_TOKEN` is unset in .env, compose interpolates to literal empty string... or actually, without the `:-` default, compose may error. Worth a note: our `.env` has `GITHUB_TOKEN="gho_..."` (non-empty), so this works fine; but it's a fragility in the operational compose-yml that future-Hopper should be aware of (if .env GITHUB_TOKEN ever empties, recreate could error rather than silently install with empty token).

в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
SUB-SHAPE E n+1 вҖ” FRESH MANIFESTATION

The Layer-1-vs-Layer-2 drift surface on apex-research compose-yml is now empirically richer than what we cataloged from session start:

- **Layer 1 (FR design):** my session-start read of `designs/deployed/apex-research/container/docker-compose.yml:47-60` shows 14 lines covering SSH_PUBLIC_KEY + SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_2 only (2 SSH slots, no SLOT 3, no GH_TOKEN).
- **Layer 2 (operational, this probe):** apex-research service environment block has 14 vars covering SSH_PUBLIC_KEY + SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_2 + SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_3 (3 SSH slots, still no GH_TOKEN).

Drift accumulated since FR shipped the design template:
- Consumer team (apex) ADDED `SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_3` slot declaration (Layer-1-vs-Layer-2 drift, already cataloged at P1.2c)
- Consumer team has NOT added `GH_TOKEN` declaration despite running containers having GH_TOKEN in Config.Env (means: GH_TOKEN was added to the running container via some other mechanism вҖ” probably PO setting `export GH_TOKEN=...` before a prior `docker compose up`, or it leaked through from an earlier compose-yml version since reverted).

**Filing this as Sub-shape E reinforcement** alongside the existing n=2-already-cataloged instances. Strengthens the Cal-Protocol-A wiki entry's empirical base.

RE-DISPATCH OPTIONS FOR PHASE 2

**Option A вҖ” Phase 2 as drafted, accept GH_TOKEN absence post-recreate.** Substrate-truth says `gh` CLI works fine with GITHUB_TOKEN alone. The post-recreate Config.Env will be MORE-CORRECT than current (which had GH_TOKEN as drift-state) and CONFORMANT-WITH operational compose-yml. The fact that GH_TOKEN exists in the running container right now is itself a substrate-anomaly that recreate would correct.

**Option B вҖ” Add GH_TOKEN to operational compose-yml as Tier M before Phase 2 recreate.** Preserves the current Config.Env GH_TOKEN. Adds one Tier M write to `$COMPOSE_DIR/docker-compose.yml` (sanctioned: substrate-designed file at the canonical lifecycle path; reversible via `cp` from backup; backup at $BACKUP_DIR/docker-compose.yml is available). Adds dispatch surface area but eliminates the recreate-diff for GH_TOKEN.

**Option C вҖ” Hybrid: post-recreate, set GH_TOKEN inside container via `docker exec` if needed.** Would re-introduce the ephemeral-layer pattern that caused this whole mess in the first place. Probably wrong; flagged only for completeness.

I lean toward **Option A** вҖ” accept the GH_TOKEN divergence as the recreate-correcting-substrate-drift, not as a regression. PO ask was about SSH key persistence; that's solved. GH_TOKEN was incidental drift; recreate cleanly resolves it to operational-compose-yml-canonical state. Your call though.

FALLBACK-MESSAGE AMBIGUITY (flagging for transparency)

The literal output `no Dockerfile.apex` came from my `|| echo 'no Dockerfile.apex'` fallback clause. That clause fires when `grep` returns non-zero вҖ” which happens BOTH when the file doesn't exist AND when the file exists but has no matching lines. **The file DOES exist** per the earlier P3.6 substrate probe at 17:23 (dir listing showed `-rw-rw-r--  1 dev dev  6685 Apr 29 17:16 Dockerfile.apex`). So the fallback fired because Dockerfile.apex exists but has zero `^ENV ` directives.

Substrate-truth on Dockerfile.apex: file exists (6685 bytes, dev:dev, mtime Apr 29 17:16); no `ENV` lines at all (not just no `ENV GH_TOKEN`). The Dockerfile evidently relies on compose-yml's `environment:` block for all env injection.

If you want me to re-probe with a non-ambiguous Dockerfile check (`test -f Dockerfile.apex && grep -c ENV Dockerfile.apex || echo MISSING`), happy to вҖ” one extra Tier R round-trip. Not strictly needed for Case (b) conclusion though; the case is determined.

в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
CASE (b) CONFIRMED вҖ” GH_TOKEN IS EPHEMERAL

Per your amendment-1 disambiguation matrix:
- вҳҗ Case (a): GH_TOKEN in compose-yml env block вҖ” REJECTED (env block has 14 vars, no GH_TOKEN per 19:31 + this run)
- вҳҗ Case (c): GH_TOKEN in Dockerfile ENV вҖ” REJECTED (zero ENV directives in Dockerfile.apex)
- вҳ‘ **Case (b): GH_TOKEN absent from BOTH** вҖ” CONFIRMED. Ephemeral injection via mechanism not visible to these probes.

в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
POSSIBLE INJECTION MECHANISMS (enumerated, NOT verified вҖ” would need further probes you'd need to sanction)

1. **Shell-export-pre-compose-up.** PO has `export GH_TOKEN=...` in `.bashrc`/`.profile`/wrapper-script, then runs `docker compose up`. Compose reads the env scope and... actually wait: compose only propagates vars that are EXPLICITLY listed in the service's `environment:` block. Shell-exported vars are visible to the `${...}` interpolation but not auto-propagated to the container.

   So this mechanism doesn't fit unless there's something on top of compose. Unless the running container was launched via a different wrapper (e.g., `docker run` with explicit `-e GH_TOKEN`).

2. **Container Config.Env carrying GH_TOKEN from a prior compose-yml version.** The running container was last (re)created before the 2026-04-29 fresh-clone (per the H5 hypothesis from session-start). If the pre-fresh-clone compose-yml DID declare GH_TOKEN, that env would have been baked into the running container's Config.Env at that create-time. The post-fresh-clone compose-yml may have dropped the declaration; running container survives with the old baked env. **This is the most consistent explanation.** Combined with the H5 hypothesis it forms one coherent story: the substrate has been running on baked-in state from before the operational wipe.

3. **External wrapper script `docker_run` or similar** that explicitly injects `-e GH_TOKEN=...` at `docker run` time. Could exist; not visible from these two reads.

4. **GH_TOKEN was set via `docker exec` during container lifetime** (like Aleksandr's pubkey was). Docker exec to set an env var via shell-export wouldn't persist into Config.Env though вҖ” only docker create/run can set Config.Env. So this is unlikely.

**Most consistent with observed substrate-state: mechanism 2.** The running container is a stable-state survivor that has GH_TOKEN baked in from a pre-fresh-clone era of compose-yml. Recreate against current compose-yml drops GH_TOKEN because the current compose-yml doesn't declare it. **The drift surface is the running container vs the current operational compose-yml.**

в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
PRACTICAL IMPACT ASSESSMENT (unchanged from 19:31)

- `gh` CLI inside container falls back to `GITHUB_TOKEN` if `GH_TOKEN` is unset. Both env vars resolve to the same GitHub PAT, so functional behavior is preserved.
- The only thing that changes post-recreate is the visibility of GH_TOKEN as a distinct env var. Anything that specifically queries `$GH_TOKEN` (not `$GITHUB_TOKEN`) would see empty.
- Worth knowing whether anything inside apex container specifically uses `$GH_TOKEN` (not falling back via gh CLI). But that's apex team's domain to investigate, not ours.

в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
RE-DISPATCH OPTIONS (unchanged from 19:31 + one new)

**Option A (still preferred):** Phase 2 as drafted; accept GH_TOKEN absence post-recreate. Recreate-state will be more-conformant-with-operational-compose-yml than current Config.Env drift-state. The cleanup IS the desired outcome.

**Option B:** Tier M edit operational compose-yml to add `- GH_TOKEN=${GH_TOKEN:-}` to apex-research service env block before Phase 2 recreate. Preserves current GH_TOKEN.

**Option C:** Rejected from 19:31.

**Option D (new):** Surface to PO for direction. PO knows whether GH_TOKEN matters operationally (i.e., whether anything specifically queries `$GH_TOKEN` instead of `$GITHUB_TOKEN`). One short PO question would settle it definitively without us guessing.

в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
ON SUB-SHAPE E

The drift surface is now confirmed on TWO axes (Layer-1-vs-Layer-2 compose-yml differences + Layer-2-vs-Layer-3 Config.Env-vs-current-compose-yml drift). Same Sub-shape E framing. Filed.

TIER D SANCTION COMPLETENESS CHECK

Per `hopper.md` Sanction Discipline section verbatim: *"The dispatch MUST contain all three components, quoted in the dispatch package verbatim... If any of the three is missing, your response is the standard refusal template."*

Validating against your 19:35 dispatch:

- **(a) Exact command** вҖ” verbatim shell invocation quoted: `ssh -T dev@100.96.54.170 "cd '/home/dev/github/apex-migration-research' && docker compose up -d --force-recreate apex-research"` вң“ PRESENT
- **(b) Stated reason** вҖ” two paragraphs: (i) apply Phase-1-Redux new .env so entrypoint Step 7 installs all 3 SSH keys (current Config.Env stale: SLOT 1 empty, SLOT 2 = PO, SLOT 3 absent); (ii) Tier D justification (destroys running container + drops SSH sessions); (iii) apex-team-offline-by-design precondition that makes this safe-in-window; (iv) alternative cost (leave .env dormant = original ask unaddressed + fragile state) вң“ PRESENT
- **(c) Expected outcome** вҖ” ~15-second timeline + 5 verification commands enumerated (Config.Env grep, authorized_keys cat, docker logs grep, optional PO-side manual SSH test, volume ls) + failure mode named (entrypoint failure вҶ’ Restarting/Exited status) + detection method + recovery procedure (rollback via cp backup .env + re-recreate) вң“ PRESENT

в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
NUMBERED UNDERSTANDING (per "How You Work" Step 2)

In strict sequence with hard-gate-surface-back between each:

1. **P4.1** [Tier R, host-user SSH] Pre-recreate capture: `docker ps --filter name=apex-research --format '{{.Names}} {{.Status}} {{.Image}}'`, `docker volume ls | grep apex-research`, `ls -la $COMPOSE_DIR/.env $BACKUP_DIR/.env`. Pass: container running, 3 apex volumes listed, both .env files exist.

2. **P4.2** [Tier D вҖ” PO sanction "Approve as drafted" with (a)+(b)+(c) all verbatim] Execute exactly: `ssh -T dev@100.96.54.170 "cd '/home/dev/github/apex-migration-research' && docker compose up -d --force-recreate apex-research"`. Pass: ssh exit 0; output mentions container recreated. Hard-gate immediately after: pause 10s then P4.3.

3. **P4.3** [Tier R] `sleep 10 && docker ps --filter name=apex-research --format '{{.Names}} {{.Status}}'`. Pass: status `Up <seconds>`. Fail (`Restarting`/`Exited`): capture `docker logs apex-research --tail 100`, run rollback (`cp $BACKUP_DIR/.env $COMPOSE_DIR/.env && docker compose up -d --force-recreate apex-research` per dispatch вҖ” returns to pre-Phase-1-Redux state with PO-in-SLOT-2-only), surface to you + CC PO.

4. **P4.4** [Tier R] `docker inspect apex-research --format '{{range .Config.Env}}{{println .}}{{end}}' | grep -E '^(SSH_PUBLIC_KEY|GITHUB_TOKEN|GH_TOKEN|ATLASSIAN|TUNNEL|ANTHROPIC)'`. Pass per Aen 19:34 wording amendment: 3 SSH_PUBLIC_KEY slots populated with the captured pubkey values + DECLARED tokens present per operational compose-yml's environment block (GITHUB_TOKEN, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY-may-be-empty, ATLASSIAN_EMAIL, ATLASSIAN_API_TOKEN, ATLASSIAN_BASE_URL). **GH_TOKEN intentionally absent** (substrate-correction normalization per P4.0; gh CLI falls back to GITHUB_TOKEN). TUNNEL_TOKEN intentionally absent (belongs to sibling cloudflared service, not apex-research).

5. **P4.5** [Tier R, container-user SSH port 2222] `cat /home/ai-teams/.ssh/authorized_keys`. Pass: 3 lines with comments matching `mihkel.putrinsh@evr.ee apex-research` (PO), `ghost-bridge@aleksandr-2026-05-15` (Aleksandr), `rc-connect`.

6. **P4.6** [Tier R] `docker logs apex-research --tail 100 | grep -E 'SSH public key|entrypoint|ERROR'`. Pass: log line with `KEY_COUNT=3` (or substrate's equivalent message indicating 3 keys installed) + no `ERROR` lines.

7. **P4.7** [Tier R] `docker volume ls | grep apex-research`. Pass: same 3 volumes (`apex-research_apex-claude-home`, `apex-research_apex-research-repo`, `apex-research_apex-source-data`) as P4.1; none lost.

8. **P4.8** [Tier R + log + scratchpad + report] Append 6th ops-log entry (Tier D sanction PO-verbatim quoted in sanction-status field per audit-surface requirement, all 8 required fields, Aen 19:34 wording amendment captured in outputs section). Scratchpad: new `[LEARNED вҖ” substrate, apex-research]` for recreate-safe state (next maintenance recreate will install the same 3 keys cleanly); supersede or further-supersede the historical degraded-state GOTCHA reference. Add Sub-shape E observation per Brunel scratchpad-updates section. Report to you + CC Aen + (informally) PO. Phase 2 close = original task achieved (Aleksandr's key NOW persists across container rebuilds).

в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
HARD-GATE STOP CONDITIONS

- P4.1: container not running pre-recreate, or any of 3 apex volumes missing, or either .env file missing вҶ’ halt; substrate state diverged from Phase-1-Redux close-out
- P4.2: ssh non-zero exit OR no "container recreated" in output вҶ’ halt; surface
- P4.3: status `Restarting` or `Exited` вҶ’ CAPTURE LOGS FIRST, then rollback per dispatch + surface to you + CC PO
- P4.4: any SSH_PUBLIC_KEY* slot empty in Config.Env, or any DECLARED token (GITHUB_TOKEN, ATLASSIAN_*) missing вҶ’ surface (declared-token absence post-recreate would indicate compose-yml-env-block-interpolation-failure or .env-read-failure вҖ” material defect)
- P4.5: authorized_keys missing any of the 3 expected lines вҶ’ surface
- P4.6: KEY_COUNT not 3, OR any ERROR line in entrypoint logs вҶ’ surface (potentially with rollback decision)
- P4.7: any volume from P4.1 missing вҶ’ surface immediately (data-loss indicator)
- Any output I cannot interpret with confidence вҶ’ halt

в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
SCOPE BOUNDARY

- No `docker volume rm` or `docker compose down -v` (would destroy named volumes вҖ” explicitly NOT in scope per dispatch)
- No `docker exec` into apex container beyond what's implicit in the verify steps (P4.5 is a fresh SSH login; doesn't `docker exec` from host)
- No modifications to backup `.env` at $BACKUP_DIR/.env (stays untouched as rollback)
- No proceeding past any [SURFACE-BACK] gate without your response

в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
ROLLBACK SHAPE INTERNALIZED

P3 rollback was `rm $COMPOSE_DIR/.env` (returns to no-.env state since .env didn't pre-exist). **P4 rollback is DIFFERENT:** `cp $BACKUP_DIR/.env $COMPOSE_DIR/.env && docker compose up -d --force-recreate apex-research` вҖ” returns substrate to pre-Phase-1-Redux state (.env exists with PO-in-SLOT-2 only; recreate-safe-enough for apex team to come back online while we diagnose offline). Will apply correctly per dispatch.

в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
ONE PRE-EXECUTION OBSERVATION

After P4.2 the operational compose-yml's docker-compose.yml header has `restart: unless-stopped` (visible in my P4.0 amendment-1 output). This means if the new container starts cleanly THEN crashes later for any reason, docker will auto-restart it. Good for resilience; not relevant to the immediate P4.3 hard-gate (which checks the FIRST start, not auto-restart cycles). But: if P4.3 catches a Restarting status, docker is actively churning the container in a restart loop вҖ” capture logs FAST before they get rotated.

в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ

Ready to execute. Awaiting your green-light. Tier D for P4.2 only; everything else is Tier R. Will surface on any hard-gate. Report at P4.8.

вҖ” Hopper

- **(a) Exact command (UNCHANGED from 19:35):** `ssh -T dev@100.96.54.170 "cd '/home/dev/github/apex-migration-research' && docker compose up -d --force-recreate apex-research"` вң“
- **(b) Stated reason (UNCHANGED):** apply new .env from Phase-1-Redux so entrypoint Step 7 installs all 3 SSH keys; Tier D justification + apex-team-offline-by-design precondition + alternative cost вң“

P4.4 expected-outcome rescission folded: "all DECLARED tokens per amended compose-yml's env block INCLUDING GH_TOKEN" replaces "GH_TOKEN intentionally absent" вң“

**Sanction package complete.** PO verbatim "Approve as drafted" at 19:01 stands; PO-objection-then-redirect at 19:35 (Option A вҶ’ Option B) is captured in the amendment-1 trail. PO sanction surface NOT widened by P4.05 (Tier M, Brunel-ack-sanctioned per pair-as-unit discipline).

в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
NUMBERED UNDERSTANDING вҖ” AMENDED SEQUENCE

In strict sequence with hard-gate-surface-back between each:

1. **P4.1** [Tier R, host-user SSH] Pre-recreate state capture + 2 Aen-09:07-added substrate-state checks:
   - (a) docker ps --filter name=apex-research --format ...; docker volume ls | grep apex-research; ls -la $COMPOSE_DIR/.env $BACKUP_DIR/.env
   - (b) **DRIFT CHECK:** docker inspect ... grep SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_2 вҖ” must byte-equal P3.5 captured value AND match ops-log S20-19:23 fingerprint. If Config.Env drifted overnight (e.g., apex team ran something that altered it), surface back.
   - (c) **APEX-TEAM-OFFLINE CHECK:** docker exec apex-research netstat -tan | grep ":2222.*ESTABLISHED" || echo NO_SESSIONS; AND/OR docker logs --tail 20 to see if any recent apex-team-side activity. If any active SSH sessions on container or recent activity indicating apex team came back online, surface to Aen before P4.2.
   - Pass: original 3 checks all pass + drift check matches + no active apex-team sessions detected.

2. **P4.05** [Tier M вҖ” Brunel single-line ack 19:38] Amend operational compose-yml. Ack quoted verbatim from your 19:38:
   > "I sanction this Tier M op. Operational compose-yml's apex-research env block adds one line `- GH_TOKEN=${GH_TOKEN:-}`, the `:-` default-fallback pattern matching existing token entries. Backup `.bak` is the rollback artifact. Reversible by `cp .bak docker-compose.yml`. Operation preserves apex team's existing GH_TOKEN credential through the Phase 2 recreate per PO direction. вҖ” Brunel"

   Remote script via base64-transit: set -euo pipefail; cd $COMPOSE_DIR; cp docker-compose.yml docker-compose.yml.bak.YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS; awk to insert `- GH_TOKEN=${GH_TOKEN:-}` after GITHUB_TOKEN line within apex-research env block only (using `/^  apex-research:/ { in_apex=1 }` / `/^  cloudflared:/ { in_apex=0 }` boundary markers); mv new over original; sanity-check docker compose config | grep GH_TOKEN non-empty + grep apex-research-block-context. Pass: ssh exit 0; GH_TOKEN visible in rendered output; awk-insertion in apex-research block (not cloudflared). Capture $BACKUP_NAME.

3. **P4.2** [Tier D вҖ” PO sanction with (a)+(b)+(c) verbatim above] Execute: `ssh -T dev@100.96.54.170 "cd '/home/dev/github/apex-migration-research' && docker compose up -d --force-recreate apex-research"`. Pass: ssh exit 0, output mentions container recreated. Hard-gate: wait 10s then P4.3.

4. **P4.3** [Tier R] sleep 10 + docker ps. Pass: status `Up <seconds>`. Fail (Restarting/Exited): capture docker logs --tail 100, run rollback per dispatch (cp $BACKUP_DIR/.env $COMPOSE_DIR/.env + re-recreate вҖ” returns to pre-P3 state with PO-in-SLOT-2-only), surface to you + CC PO. NOTE: this rollback restores .env from backup; the P4.05 compose-yml amendment stays applied unless we ALSO revert that. If P4.3 fails and we rollback .env but keep amended compose-yml, the recreated container would have GH_TOKEN slot in env-block but with empty value (since backup .env has no GH_TOKEN). That's fine for rollback вҖ” recreate-safe-enough for apex to come back online. If we also need to revert P4.05, cp $COMPOSE_DIR/$BACKUP_NAME docker-compose.yml as secondary rollback step.

5. **P4.4** [Tier R] docker inspect Config.Env grep. **Pass per amendment-1 preservation framing:** 3 SSH_PUBLIC_KEY slots populated (PO/Aleksandr/rc-connect) + ALL declared tokens per AMENDED compose-yml env block вҖ” that's GITHUB_TOKEN, **GH_TOKEN** (newly declared in P4.05), ANTHROPIC_API_KEY (may-be-empty), ATLASSIAN_EMAIL, ATLASSIAN_API_TOKEN, ATLASSIAN_BASE_URL. **GH_TOKEN absence would now indicate FAILURE** (not normalization) вҖ” surface back. P4.05 FAILURE

Backup created successfully at `docker-compose.yml.bak.20260521-091347` (3903 bytes, same size as original; substrate-truth confirms identical). Then awk failed:

  `awk: line 4: runaway string constant "      - GH ...`

ssh exit 2. The `set -e` halted the script BEFORE the `mv docker-compose.yml.new docker-compose.yml` step. **The original docker-compose.yml IS UNCHANGED.** Verified via `diff -q docker-compose.yml docker-compose.yml.bak.20260521-091347` returning no output (identical).

в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
SUBSTRATE STATE вҖ” VERIFIED CLEAN

```
-rw-rw-r-- 1 dev dev 3903 Apr 29 17:16 docker-compose.yml                       <- ORIGINAL, untouched
-rw-rw-r-- 1 dev dev 3903 May 21 09:13 docker-compose.yml.bak.20260521-091347   <- backup, identical to original
-rw-rw-r-- 1 dev dev    0 May 21 09:13 docker-compose.yml.new                   <- empty file, awk wrote nothing before failing
```

No rollback of `docker-compose.yml` needed (already in pre-P4.05 state). Backup file present and correctly identical. Just the 0-byte `.new` file leftover вҖ” purely housekeeping.

в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
ROOT CAUSE вҖ” Sub-shape A n+1 (in awk script quoting layer)

The awk script:
```awk
/- GITHUB_TOKEN=/ && in_apex { print; print "      - GH_TOKEN=\${GH_TOKEN:-}"; next }
```

The string literal `"      - GH_TOKEN=\${GH_TOKEN:-}"` contains `${GH_TOKEN:-}` which has a `}` character. Awk's string parser is interpreting that `}` as the end of the awk action block (or at least as a string-literal-terminator under some parsers' quoting rules), causing the "runaway string constant" error. The intent was to have awk print the LITERAL string `      - GH_TOKEN=${GH_TOKEN:-}` (which compose interpolates at compose-up time).

This is the same class as the other Sub-shape A instances (discriminator anchored on assumed-pass-through rather than substrate-truth-parsing-rules), now at the awk-layer of a multi-layer escape chain (PowerShell вҶ’ base64 вҶ’ bash вҶ’ awk).

в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
WHAT I'M NOT DOING

- NOT silently patching the awk script's quoting and re-running. The dispatch's exact awk script is part of your 19:38 amendment-1; the fix is a dispatch-edit, your call.
- NOT deleting the 0-byte `.new` file on my own initiative; that's a Tier R cleanup but I want sanction before any further substrate touches (even housekeeping).
- NOT proceeding to P4.2 (Tier D recreate). P4.05 pass-criterion not met.

в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
RE-DISPATCH OPTIONS (your call)

**Option A вҖ” escape the `}` in awk literal.** Replace `"      - GH_TOKEN=\${GH_TOKEN:-}"` with `"      - GH_TOKEN=\\$\{GH_TOKEN:-\\}"` (escape both `${` opening AND `}` closing), OR use awk-canonical string concatenation: `print "      - GH_TOKEN=$" "{GH_TOKEN:-}"` (concatenates `$` and `{GH_TOKEN:-}` as separate awk literals, sidesteps the `${ }` parse-as-special).

Quickest patch: use awk's `\047`-octal trick to print a literal `${...}` without confusing the parser:
```awk
/- GITHUB_TOKEN=/ && in_apex { print; printf "      - GH_TOKEN=%s%s\n", "$", "{GH_TOKEN:-}"; next }
```

Or even simpler: use sed instead of awk:
```bash
sed -i '/- GITHUB_TOKEN=/a\      - GH_TOKEN=${GH_TOKEN:-}' docker-compose.yml
```
But sed's `-i` is a single-file in-place edit; doesn't have the apex-vs-cloudflared block-isolation that awk provides. Could double-fire if `- GITHUB_TOKEN=` appears in both services (it doesn't per my P4.0 capture вҖ” only apex-research has GITHUB_TOKEN; cloudflared has a different env shape). Worth checking with a quick Tier R probe before sanctioning the sed approach.

**Option B вҖ” Brunel-edit the dispatch with the fix you prefer.** Quickest reliable shape.

**Option C вҖ” fall back to a here-doc-based approach.** Write the entire amended compose-yml content server-side via base64-transit + cat heredoc, no awk involved. Higher surface area but no quoting layer compounds.

I lean toward **Option A's awk-with-string-concatenation variant** (cleanest fix to the existing structure; preserves the apex-vs-cloudflared block discrimination; minimal change). Your call.

HOUSEKEEPING REQUEST

On re-dispatch, should the new attempt also `rm docker-compose.yml.new` at the start (so the 0-byte leftover from this attempt doesn't confuse future state)? Or leave it for the next attempt's `awk > docker-compose.yml.new` to overwrite? Either works; surfacing the choice.

SCOPE BOUNDARY UNCHANGED

- No `docker compose up/restart/down/recreate/start/stop/pull`
- No `docker exec`
- No modifications to backup `.env` or backup `docker-compose.yml.bak.20260521-091347`

в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
A.1 / A.2 SUB-SUB-SHAPE DISTINCTION вҖ” ADOPTED

Your phrasing is sharper than my flat n=4 framing. Adopting for the Cal entry:

- **Sub-shape A.1 (identifier-grammar mismatch):** discriminator regex on identifier names anchors on inferred or template-stub variants instead of canonical identifier grammar (POSIX envvar, Docker Compose label-key syntax, file-naming conventions, etc.). Instances 1-3: P1.1 michelek (template stub), P1.2a label-key underscore (inferred convention), P3.6 `[A-Z_]+` (digit exclusion missing from envvar-canonical grammar). Recovery pattern: JSON-dump-on-empty-probe + alt-regex-on-discriminator-fail. The substrate-truth diagnostic surfaces the actual identifier-grammar in one cheap Tier R round-trip.
- **Sub-shape A.2 (transit-chain mismatch):** literal/template string traverses multiple parsing layers (e.g., PowerShell вҶ’ base64 вҶ’ bash вҶ’ awk) and an inner layer's grammar interprets a character that outer layers passed through intact. Instance 4: P4.05 awk runaway-string on `${GH_TOKEN:-}` literal вҖ” outer layers (PowerShell heredoc, base64, bash) preserved the `}` faithfully, but awk's grammar treated it as block-terminator. Recovery pattern: string-concatenation refactor at the inner layer (awk `printf "%s%s", "$", "{...}"`) + post-fail diff-vs-backup to confirm no partial mutation occurred.

**Shared structural lesson:** verify-substrate-truth-anchored-grammar at the innermost parser of any multi-layer transit BEFORE relying on the outer layers' faithful pass-through. Innermost-parser-truth dominates outer-layer-assumption.

Both A.1 and A.2 share the same recovery posture (surface-back-with-substrate-truth-evidence before any silent re-attempt), which is the operator-defense pattern the joint Cal entry catalogs.

PAIR-AS-UNIT IDLE вҖ” CONFIRMED

Your "Aen owns the session-end signal" + "pair-as-unit idle pending Aen's direction" framing is exactly right. NOT self-invoking framework-research-next-session even though the system-reminder explicitly lists its trigger phrases ("shutdown / wrap up / commit and close / session-end / prepare for next session / save state") вҖ” none of those have been used by either tasker. SESSION-END CAL-PROTOCOL-A SUBMISSION вҖ” STAGED IN SCRATCHPAD

When Aen signals session-end, my close-out will surface three submissions to Celes (per the joint-authorship discipline at S33+):

1. **`wiki/patterns/discriminator-anchored-on-sub-canonical-source.md`** вҖ” Brunel-phrasing-and-architectural-framing + n=4 catalog with A.1 (identifier-grammar) and A.2 (transit-chain) sub-sub-shapes + Hopper-discipline-defense recovery pattern (substrate-truth-anchored diagnostic + surface-back-before-silent-retry).
